corrections.note: Printed text reads συνέμιγνυν, not συνἐμετριγον.

Accent should be grave before following word in print: ἐφνίδιον → ἐφνίδιον? Actually image shows ἐφνίδιον without acute? No correction needed except transcript has ν not correct? Printed word is ἐφνίδιον.
Printed text has ἀφασίαν with grave? No substantive difference; disregard if normalization policy applied.
Printed text has ἣν? likely ἣν; no real difference.
Body omits prefix δι- and initial accenting; printed text reads διόπερ ἔπεμψεν.
Apparatus reading in image appears συνήγεις (sic) A, not συνηγορει.
Transcript has stray 'περι 20] υπερ V' carried from line break; apparatus at this point reads κρισιν] υπευλαβειτο επεν. Vvid.

uncertain.note: Several accent marks in transcript may differ from print, but not reported confidently because they are minor and image resolution is limited.
The A reading after συνηγαγεν is difficult; looks like συνήγεις (sic) A, but small type is not perfectly clear.

notes: Running head and page number match. Main body is largely correct, but there is a clear misread at v. 14 and omission of διόπερ at v. 19. Apparatus mostly matches, but one line-break corruption around v. 18 and one probable misread at v. 16 are present.
